The refining processes at the Lake Charles Refinery include atmospheric distillation and fluid catalytic cracking (FCC). In atmospheric distillation, crude oil is heated and separated into different fractions based on their boiling points. FCC breaks down heavier fractions of crude oil into lighter, more valuable products such as gasoline and diesel fuel.
